name: Polish Sausage
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut pork into 1 1/2-inch cubes, trimming all gristle and bone.
2. Pass the meat through a meat grinder with a coarse blade.
3. Adjust the fat-to-lean ratio to be about 1:3 if possible.
4. Place the pork in a large stainless or ceramic crock or bowl.
5. Mix dry spices in a small bowl.
6. Using your hands, toss the meat while gradually adding the spices, a small amount at a time.
7. When half the spices are incorporated, add half the ice water.
8. Continue mixing to keep the meat loose.
9. Add remaining spices and water as described above.
10. Optionally, fry a small patty of the meat to test for seasonings; adjust if necessary.
11. Refrigerate the sausage mix overnight.
12. You may check for seasonings again the next day (but be cautious—you might want to cook and eat it right away!).
13. Stuff the mixture into about 5 feet of rinsed casings, tying off approximately every 8 inches.
14. Cook the sausages by grilling, steaming, or frying as desired.
